case_metadata.case_name: Candelario v. MJHS Hospice & Palliative Care, Inc.

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Defendants failed to demonstrate, prima facie, either the absence of any departure for the accepted medical practice, or that any departure was not a proximate cause of the plaintiffs' injuries
evidence_index.summary: Defendants did not show absence of departure from medical practice or lack of causation.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: According to defendants' expert, the applicable standard of care required an emergency medicine physician to evaluate the patient, take a medical history, and determine an appropriate disposition.
evidence_index.summary: Defendants' expert stated standard of care involves evaluation, history taking, and disposition.
evidence_index.speaker: Defendants' expert

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: There is conflicting evidence as to whether defendant Steven Winograd, M.D., departed from accepted practice by improperly recording plaintiff's self-reported pancreatic cancer as stage IV, resulting in plaintiff's transfer to hospice.
evidence_index.summary: Conflicting evidence on whether Dr. Winograd improperly recorded cancer stage.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Nothing in the record supports defendants' contention that plaintiff informed Dr. Winograd that his pancreatic cancer was at stage IV, which plaintiff explicitly denied at his deposition, testifying that defendants staged his cancer.
evidence_index.summary: Plaintiff denied informing Dr. Winograd of stage IV cancer; claims defendants staged it.
evidence_index.speaker: Plaintiff

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Plaintiffs' expert opined that the standard of care required emergency room physicians to, among other things, order an oncology consult when a patient reported a history of pancreatic cancer, which defendants did not do.
evidence_index.summary: Plaintiffs' expert stated standard of care required oncology consult, which was not done.
evidence_index.speaker: Plaintiffs' expert

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Plaintiffs' expert further opined that it was a departure for Dr. Winograd not to confirm that plaintiff had stage IV pancreatic cancer before referring him to hospice.
evidence_index.summary: Expert opined it was a departure not to confirm cancer stage before hospice referral.
evidence_index.speaker: Plaintiffs' expert

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Plaintiff remained for over 18 months under the false premise that he required end of life care.
evidence_index.summary: Plaintiff stayed in hospice for over 18 months under false end-of-life care premise.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Summary judgment dismissing the loss of consortium claim was properly denied.
evidence_index.summary: Loss of consortium claim dismissal was denied.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Mrs. Candelario testified that despite living apart plaintiffs continued to provide each other with practical and emotional support, affection, and companionship.
evidence_index.summary: Mrs. Candelario testified about continued support and companionship despite living apart.
evidence_index.speaker: Mrs. Candelario

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Summary judgment dismissing plaintiff's cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress was also properly denied.
evidence_index.summary: Dismissal of negligent infliction of emotional distress claim was denied.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

verdict_detected.key_phrase: Defendants failed to demonstrate, prima facie, either the absence of any departure for the accepted medical practice, or that any departure was not a proximate cause of the plaintiffs' injuries

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient reported a history of pancreatic cancer, which was recorded as stage IV by Dr. Winograd.

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

critical_findings: Stage IV cancer recorded without confirmation

requestables.name: Oncology Consult
requestables.description: Consultation with oncology to confirm cancer stage

reveal.clinical_significance: Would have confirmed or refuted stage IV diagnosis
reveal.decision_impact: Critical for appropriate management and referral

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ED with reported history of pancreatic cancer
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in managing this patient?

options.description: Order oncology consult to confirm cancer stage
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard of care requires confirmation of cancer stage before making end-of-life care decisions

options.description: Record stage IV cancer and refer to hospice
options.clinical_reasoning: Assumed stage IV based on unverified information

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant recorded stage IV cancer and referred to hospice
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Assumed stage IV based on patient report

expected_action_court.description: Oncology consult should have been ordered
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established need for confirmation

explanation.what_should_have_happened: Oncology consult to confirm cancer stage

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with a history of pancreatic cancer

key_events.event: Patient reported history of pancreatic cancer

patient_outcome.description: Patient remained in hospice for over 18 months under false premise of end-of-life care

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to confirm cancer stage

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to confirm cancer stage before hospice referral
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Oncology consult would have clarified cancer stage

factual_timeline.event: Patient reported history of pancreatic cancer
